name: Penanggalan
description: The Penanggalan is a vampiric entity originating from Malaysian folklore. It appears as a disembodied female head with its organs and entrails dangling below. By day, it may resemble an ordinary woman, often a midwife. However, at night, it detaches its head from its body and flies in search of blood, particularly that of pregnant women and newborn babies. The trailing organs glow in the dark, and it leaves a trail of viscous fluid wherever it goes. It is a fearsome creature, preying on the vulnerable and embodying the dangers lurking in the shadows. The Penanggalan is said to be created through the misuse of magical practices or a curse. Some stories say they were once beautiful women who made a pact with dark forces, and others claim they were simply wicked individuals who practiced forbidden rituals to gain immortality. Regardless of its origin, the Penanggalan is a symbol of terror and a cautionary tale against dabbling in the occult. It is believed to be able to pass through small cracks and crevices, making it difficult to keep out of a home. The creature's presence is often indicated by a sour vinegar smell. Its victims often suffer from a wasting disease, and if the Penanggalan manages to consume a newborn's blood, the child will likely die.
subtitle: Floating head with trailing entrails
appearance: A disembodied female head with long, flowing black hair. Its entrails and internal organs dangle beneath, glowing with an eerie light. The head is often described as beautiful and alluring, masking its true nature.
habitat: Rural villages and areas near forests, particularly in Southeast Asia (Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand). They are often found near homes with pregnant women or newborns.
behavior: Nocturnal predator that hunts for blood, primarily targeting pregnant women and newborn babies. It flies through the air, using its trailing organs to navigate and sense its prey. It is cunning and deceptive, often appearing as an ordinary woman during the day.
weaknesses: Iron nails (to seal the empty body)

keyMyths.title: The Empty Body
keyMyths.description: When the Penanggalan detaches its head, its body remains behind. If the empty body is filled with thorny vines or iron nails, the head cannot reattach, and the Penanggalan will die. This is a common method of defeating the creature.

keyMyths.title: The Vinegar Trap
keyMyths.description: Penanggalan are repelled by vinegar. Placing vinegar-soaked cloths or containers of vinegar around a home can deter the creature from entering and prevent it from attacking. This is a common preventative measure.

sources.author: Rosemary Ellen Guiley
sources.work: The Encyclopedia of Demons and Demonology
sources.description: Provides a comprehensive overview of demonic entities, including the Penanggalan.
sources.text: Provides a comprehensive overview of demonic entities, including the Penanggalan.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: Collection of traditional stories and beliefs about the Penanggalan passed down through generations.
sources.text: Collection of traditional stories and beliefs about the Penanggalan passed down through generations.
